title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government is issuing a response to a report on artificial intelligence and public standards. The response outlines measures the government plans to take in light of the report's recommendations.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This report assesses the impact of using copyrighted materials to develop artificial intelligence systems. It provides an analysis of the copyright implications for AI development.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The Department for Education's policy on generative AI in education outlines their stance on its use within the education sector. The purpose of the policy is to provide guidance and clarify the department's position on this increasingly prevalent technology.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: This transport action plan outlines how a government department will work with the transport sector to safely use and benefit from artificial intelligence (AI). The plan aims to address the chances and risks associated with AI in the transport industry.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This is a progress report on how copyright works are being used in the development of AI systems. It aims to address the guidelines outlined in Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The UK government is responding to recommendations on how to regulate artificial intelligence used in medical devices. This response aims to ensure AI medical devices are safe and effective for patients.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: This document outlines Ofqual's approach to controlling and overseeing the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector. It responds to government requests for guidance on regulating AI's role in education.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government has responded to a report on artificial intelligence, expressing concerns and a need for continued improvement. The response outlines government actions to address the issues and recommendations presented in the 'AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency' report.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: This document outlines the terms of reference and frequently asked questions for a committee reviewing how artificial intelligence intersects with and influences public standards. It guides the committee's work in understanding AI's impact on societal norms and policies.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The UK's Committee on Standards in Public Life (CSPL) has provided written evidence to a committee investigating how to govern artificial intelligence. They are contributing to the discussion on the government's role in overseeing AI.
